LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANT GUY ENGLEMANN'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT IS GRANTED ON THE GROUND THAT THE CLAIMS ASSERTED AGAINST HIM ARE BARRED BY THE APPLICABLE STATUTE OF LIMITATIONS. THE UNDISPUTED FACTS (DEFENDANT ENGLEMANN'S UNDISPUTED MATERIAL FACTS 1-4) ESTABLISH THE FOLLOWING: PLAINTIFF DAGEL'S CLAIM FOR MEDICAL NEGLIGENCE AGAINST DR. ENGELMANN WAS FILED ON JULY 9, 2013. BACK SURGERY WAS PERFORMED ON PLAINTIFF DAGEL ON JUNE 1, 2012 AT ST. FRANCIS MEMORIAL HOSPITAL, AND DR. ENGELMANN WAS THE ASSISTANT SURGEON (DEFENDANT'S EX. D, JUNE 1, 2012 OPERATIVE REPORT). PLAINTIFF STARTED BELIEVING ON JUNE 1ST AND JUNE 2ND, 2012 THAT HE WAS RECEIVING NEGLECTFUL CARE AT ST. FRANCIS. (DEFENDANT'S EX. C, DAGEL DEPOSITION AT 274-276). PLAINTIFF DID NOT SERVE DR. ENGELMANN WITH A CCP 364.90 DAY NOTICE OF INTENT. (ID. AT 278:3-5.) THE APPLICABLE STATUTE OF LIMITATIONS UNDER THESE CIRCUMSTANCES IS CCP 340.5 ("ONE YEAR AFTER THE PLAINTIFF DISCOVERS, OR THROUGH THE USE OF REASONABLE DILIGENCE, SHOULD HAVE DISCOVERED, THE INJURY"). PLAINTIFF HAS FILED A STATEMENT OF NON-OPPOSITION TO THE MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT. CO-DEFENDANT LIGHT'S OPPOSITION TO THE SUMMARY JUDGMENT MOTION DID NOT DISPUTE UNDISPUTED MATERIAL FACTS 1-4 OR ADDRESS WHETHER THE CLAIMS ASSERTED AGAINST DEFENDANT ENGLEMANN ARE BARRED BY THE STATUTE OF LIMITATIONS. IN LIGHT OF THE FOREGOING, THE COURT DOES NOT REACH THE ALTERNATIVE GROUNDS FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT OFFERED BY DEFENDANT ENGLEMANN. CO-DEFENDANT LIGHT'S EVIDENTIARY OBJECTIONS TO THE DECLARATION OF DR. POTTORFF AND REQUEST TO STRIKE THE DECLARATION OF DR. POTTORFF ARE DENIED AS MOOT. THE COURT DOES NOT REACH THE ISSUE OF WHETHER CO-DEFENDANT LIGHT EVEN HAD STANDING TO OPPOSE DEFENDANT ENGLEMANN'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT. ORDER SIGNED. JUDGE: MARLA J. MILLER; CLERK: GINA GONZALES; SHERYL L. SAWYER CSR# 5976.(302/MJM)

Prevailing party to prepare a form of order pursuant to CCP 437 C(g). Judge: Ernest H. Goldsmith; Clerk: Cynthia Herbert; Reporter: Sheryl L. Sawyer, CSR # 5976 =302/ehg

MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT
Off Calendar Oct-17-2014 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Mary M. Sackett, Esq. of The Goldman Law Firm, T:415.435.5500, for defendant and moving party;
No other appearance.

RULING: The tentative ruling is not contested and adopted. DEFENDANT GUY ENGLEMANN's MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT is granted on the ground that the claims asserted against him are barred by the applicable statute of limitations. The undisputed facts (Defendant Englemann's Undisputed Material Facts 1-4) establish the following: Plaintiff Dagel's claim for medical negligence against Dr. Engelmann was filed on July 9, 2013. Back surgery was performed on Plaintiff Dagel on June 1, 2012 at St. Francis Memorial Hospital, and Dr. Engelmann was the assistant surgeon (Defendant's Ex. D, June 1, 2012 operative report). Plaintiff started believing on June 1st and June 2nd, 2012 that he was receiving neglectful care at St. Francis. (Defendant's Ex. C, Dagel deposition at 274-276). Plaintiff did not serve Dr. Engelmann with a CCP 364.90 day notice of intent. (Id. at 278:3-5.) The applicable statute of limitations under these circumstances is CCP 340.5 ("one year after the plaintiff discovers, or through the use of reasonable diligence, should have discovered, the injury"). Plaintiff has filed a statement of non-opposition to the motion for summary judgment. Co-Defendant Light's opposition to the summary judgment motion did not dispute Undisputed Material Facts 1-4 or address whether the claims asserted against Defendant Englemann are barred by the statute of limitations.

In light of the foregoing, the Court does not reach the alternative grounds for summary judgment offered by Defendant Englemann. Co-Defendant Light's evidentiary objections to the Declaration of Dr. Pottorff and request to strike the Declaration of Dr. Pottorff are denied as moot. The Court does not reach the issue of whether co-Defendant Light even had standing to oppose Defendant Englemann's motion for summary judgment. ORDER SIGNED. Judge: Marla J. Miller; Clerk: Gina Gonzales; Sheryl L. Sawyer CSR# 5976.
